title: Understatedly Sweet Chinese Duck Bao
description: A tangy and savory Chinese Chaozhou-inspired dish featuring a cheese-pull duck bao, perfect for date night. The addition of duck sauce and a dairy-free-friendly sauce brings a unique twist to this traditional dish.
instructions: In a small bowl, whisk together soy sauce, honey, Dijon mustard, and rice vinegar to make the duck sauce.
In a large pan, heat sesame oil over medium heat. Add the chopped scallions and cook for 2-3 minutes.
Add the grated ginger and minced garlic to the pan and cook for another minute.
Add the duck breast to the pan and cook for 3-4 minutes per side, or until cooked through.
Let the duck rest for 5 minutes before slicing it thinly.
Meanwhile, in a small bowl, mix together warm water and active dry yeast. Let it sit for 5 minutes until frothy.
Add the warm water mixture to the flour and mix until a dough forms.
Knead the dough for 5-7 minutes until smooth and elastic.
Place the dough in a greased bowl, cover it with a damp cloth, and let it rise in a warm place for 1 hour, or until doubled in size.
Preheat a pan or wok over medium heat.
Add the sliced duck to the pan and cook for 2-3 minutes, or until slightly caramelized.
Add the cooked dough to the pan and cook for 2-3 minutes, or until the dough is golden brown and the filling is heated through.
Cut the dough into 4-6 equal pieces and serve immediately.
